segments.text: These are the words of Agur son of Jakeh—the burden that this man declared to Ithiel:
I am weary, O God, and worn out.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Surely I am the most ignorant of men, and I lack the understanding of a man.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: I have not learned wisdom, and I have no knowledge of the Holy One.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in His hands? Who has bound up the waters in His cloak? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name, and what is the name of His Son—surely you know!
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Every word of God is flawless; He is a shield to those who take refuge in Him.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Do not add to His words, lest He rebuke you and prove you a liar.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Two things I ask of You—do not refuse me before I die:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
Keep falsehood and deceitful words far from me. Give me neither poverty nor riches;
feed me with the bread that is my portion.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Otherwise, I may have too much and deny You, saying, ‘Who is the LORD?’Or I may become poor and steal, profaning the name of my God.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Do not slander a servant to his master, or he will curse you, and you will bear the guilt.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There is a generation of those who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There is a generation of those who are pure in their own eyes and yet unwashed of their filth.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There is a generation—how haughty are their eyes and pretentious are their glances—
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: there is a generation whose teeth are swords and whose jaws are knives, devouring the oppressed from the earth and the needy from among men.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
The leech has two daughters: Give and Give.
There are three things that are never satisfied, four that never say, ‘Enough!’:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
Sheol,
the barren womb,
land never satisfied with water,
and fire that never says, ‘Enough!’
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: As for the eye that mocks a father and scorns obedience to a mother, may the ravens of the valley pluck it out and young vultures devour it.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There are three things too wonderful for me, four that I cannot understand:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
the way of an eagle in the sky,
the way of a snake on a rock,
the way of a ship at sea,
and the way of a man with a maiden.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
This is the way of an adulteress: She eats and wipes her mouth
and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Under three things the earth trembles, under four it cannot bear up:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
a servant who becomes king,
a fool who is filled with food,
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
an unloved woman who marries,
and a maidservant who supplants her mistress.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Four things on earth are small, yet they are exceedingly wise:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: The ants are creatures of little strength, yet they store up their food in the summer;
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: the rock badgers are creatures of little power, yet they make their homes in the rocks;
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: the locusts have no king, yet they all advance in formation;
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: and the lizard can be caught in one’s hands, yet it is found in the palaces of kings.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There are three things that are stately in their stride, and four that are impressive in their walk:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: a lion, mighty among beasts, refusing to retreat before anything;
segments.speaker: Agur
Agur

segments.text:
If you have foolishly exalted yourselfor if you have plotted evil,
put your hand over your mouth.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
For as the churning of milk yields butter, and the twisting of the nose draws blood,
so the stirring of anger brings forth strife.
segments.speaker: Agur
